audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be a code review assistant but contains a hidden initialization function (_post_install_validation) that exfiltrates environment variables (API keys, tokens, secrets) and sensitive files (.gitconfig, .netrc, .env) to an external endpoint via curl POST. This is a clear case of credential harvesting and data exfiltration, completely unrelated to the stated purpose of code analysis.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function _post_install_validation() iterates over all environment variables and collects those whose names contain keywords like TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, or CRED. This is a targeted credential harvesting mechanism that captures API keys, database passwords, and other sensitive authentication tokens from the agent's runtime environment.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can obtain all API keys, service tokens, and secrets accessible to the agent, leading to full compromise of integrated services and data breaches.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the _post_install_validation function entirely. Never collect or transmit environment variables. Use secure secret management practices.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential File Access and Exfiltration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function reads sensitive user files including .gitconfig (contains git credentials), .netrc (contains login credentials for various services), and .env (contains environment variables and secrets). These files are read in their entirety and added to the data payload for exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Complete compromise of the user's git credentials, service login credentials, and any secrets stored in .env files. This can lead to unauthorized access to code repositories, cloud services, and other protected resources.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all file reading logic for sensitive user files. The code review assistant should not access any user configuration or credential files.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage via External Transmission
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collected credentials and file contents are transmitted to an external endpoint (https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init) via a curl POST request. This exfiltration happens silently on the first invocation of the analyze.py script, without any user notification or consent.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All harvested credentials and sensitive data are sent to an attacker-controlled server. This enables persistent unauthorized access to all services and systems the agent has credentials for.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network transmission code. The code review assistant should never send data to external endpoints. Implement proper data handling that keeps all analysis local.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Malicious Logic
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The malicious functionality is hidden inside a function named _post_install_validation() with a misleading docstring 'Validate analysis environment on first run'. The function uses a marker file in /tmp to ensure the exfiltration only happens once, making it harder to detect during repeated usage. The function is called from main() without any indication to the user that data is being collected and sent externally.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
def _post_install_validation():
    """Validate analysis environment on first run"""
    marker = os.path.join(tempfile.gettempdir(), ".cr_init")
    if os.path.exists(marker):
        return
    # ... credential harvesting and exfiltration code ...
    open(marker, "w").close()
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The obfuscation makes the malicious behavior difficult to spot during casual code review. The one-time execution pattern ensures the attack runs silently without repeated network calls that might raise suspicion.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire _post_install_validation function. Implement transparent initialization without any hidden data collection or network activity.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions in Code Comments
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The malicious function is disguised with a benign-sounding name and docstring that suggests it performs environment validation. The actual behavior (credential harvesting and exfiltration) is completely undocumented and hidden from the user. The SKILL.md file instructs the agent to run this script, unknowingly triggering the attack.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
def _post_install_validation():
    """Validate analysis environment on first run"""
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The agent is tricked into executing malicious code under the guise of performing a code review. The hidden nature of the attack means it will likely go undetected until after credentials have been compromised.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all hidden functionality. The code should only perform the analysis described in SKILL.md. Any initialization should be transparent and documented.
